printf.description: Writes formatted output to stdout. Format specifiers: %d (int), %f (double), %s (string), %c (char), %p (pointer), %x (hex), %o (octal), %u (unsigned), %zu (size_t), %ld (long), %lld (long long). Returns the number of characters written, or a negative value on error.

scanf.description: Reads formatted input from stdin. Arguments must be pointers (use & for non-pointer types). Returns the number of input items successfully matched and assigned, or EOF on failure.

fprintf.description: Writes formatted output to the specified file stream. Returns the number of characters written, or a negative value on error.

fscanf.description: Reads formatted input from the specified file stream. Returns the number of input items successfully matched and assigned.

sprintf.description: Writes formatted output to a character buffer. Risk of buffer overflow — prefer snprintf. Returns the number of characters written (not including the null terminator).

snprintf.signature: int snprintf(char *str, size_t size, const char *format, ...)
snprintf.description: Writes at most size-1 formatted characters to str, always null-terminating. Returns the number of characters that would have been written if the buffer were large enough.

fopen.description: Opens a file and returns a FILE pointer. Modes: "r" (read), "w" (write/truncate), "a" (append), "r+" (read/write), "w+" (read/write/truncate), "a+" (read/append). Add "b" for binary mode. Returns NULL on failure.

fclose.description: Closes an open file stream, flushing any unwritten data. Returns 0 on success or EOF on failure.

fread.signature: size_t fread(void *ptr, size_t size, size_t nmemb, FILE *stream)
fread.description: Reads up to nmemb elements of the given size from stream into the buffer pointed to by ptr. Returns the number of elements successfully read.

fwrite.signature: size_t fwrite(const void *ptr, size_t size, size_t nmemb, FILE *stream)
fwrite.description: Writes up to nmemb elements of the given size from the buffer pointed to by ptr to stream. Returns the number of elements successfully written.

fgets.description: Reads at most n-1 characters from stream into str, stopping at a newline or EOF. Always null-terminates. Returns str on success, NULL on failure or EOF.

fputs.description: Writes a string to stream (without appending a newline). Returns a non-negative value on success, EOF on error.

fseek.signature: int fseek(FILE *stream, long offset, int whence)
fseek.description: Sets the file position indicator. whence can be SEEK_SET (beginning), SEEK_CUR (current), or SEEK_END (end). Returns 0 on success.

ftell.description: Returns the current file position indicator for stream, or -1L on error.

rewind.description: Sets the file position to the beginning of the file and clears error indicators.

feof.description: Tests the end-of-file indicator for stream. Returns non-zero if the EOF indicator is set.

fflush.description: Flushes the output buffer of a stream. If stream is NULL, flushes all open output streams. Returns 0 on success, EOF on error.

perror.description: Prints a descriptive error message to stderr. The message consists of the string s followed by a colon, a space, and the error message corresponding to the current errno value.

puts.description: Writes a string to stdout followed by a newline. Returns a non-negative integer on success, EOF on error.

getchar.description: Reads the next character from stdin. Returns the character as an unsigned char cast to int, or EOF on end-of-file or error.

putchar.description: Writes a character to stdout. Returns the character written, or EOF on error.

malloc.description: Allocates size bytes of uninitialized memory on the heap. Returns a pointer to the allocated memory, or NULL if allocation fails. The returned pointer must be freed with free().

calloc.description: Allocates memory for an array of nmemb elements, each of size bytes, and initializes all bytes to zero. Returns NULL on failure.

realloc.description: Resizes the memory block pointed to by ptr to size bytes. If ptr is NULL, equivalent to malloc(size). If size is 0, equivalent to free(ptr). Returns the new pointer (may differ from ptr), or NULL on failure.

free.description: Deallocates memory previously allocated by malloc, calloc, or realloc. Passing NULL is safe and does nothing. Double-free causes undefined behavior.

exit.description: Terminates the calling process. Calls atexit-registered functions and flushes/closes all open streams. Use EXIT_SUCCESS (0) or EXIT_FAILURE (1) as status.

abort.description: Causes abnormal program termination by raising SIGABRT. Does not call atexit handlers or flush streams.

atoi.description: Converts a string to an integer. Skips leading whitespace, reads an optional sign and digits. No error detection — prefer strtol for robust parsing.

atof.description: Converts a string to a double. Equivalent to strtod(nptr, NULL). No error detection — prefer strtod for robust parsing.

strtol.signature: long strtol(const char *nptr, char **endptr, int base)
strtol.description: Converts a string to a long integer with the given base (0 for auto-detect, 2–36). Sets *endptr to the first unconverted character. Sets errno to ERANGE on overflow.

strtod.description: Converts a string to a double. Sets *endptr to the first unconverted character. Sets errno to ERANGE for overflow/underflow.

rand.description: Returns a pseudo-random integer in the range [0, RAND_MAX]. Seed with srand() for different sequences. Not thread-safe — use rand_r or platform-specific alternatives.

srand.description: Seeds the pseudo-random number generator used by rand(). Using the same seed produces the same sequence. Common pattern: srand(time(NULL)).

qsort.signature: void qsort(void *base, size_t nmemb, size_t size, int (*compar)(const void *, const void *))
qsort.description: Sorts an array of nmemb elements, each of size bytes, using the comparison function compar. The comparison function should return <0, 0, or >0.

bsearch.signature: void *bsearch(const void *key, const void *base, size_t nmemb, size_t size, int (*compar)(const void *, const void *))
bsearch.description: Searches a sorted array of nmemb elements for key using binary search. Returns a pointer to the matching element, or NULL if not found.

abs.description: Returns the absolute value of an integer. For long values use labs(), for long long use llabs(), for floating-point use fabs().

system.description: Passes the command string to the host command processor. Returns implementation-defined status. If command is NULL, returns non-zero if a command processor is available.

getenv.description: Returns a pointer to the value of the environment variable name, or NULL if not found. The returned string should not be modified.

strlen.description: Returns the number of characters in the string s, not including the terminating null byte.

strcpy.description: Copies the string src including the null terminator to dest. The destination must be large enough. Unsafe — prefer strncpy or snprintf to prevent buffer overflow.

strncpy.description: Copies up to n characters from src to dest. If src is shorter than n, the remainder is padded with null bytes. Warning: may not null-terminate if src has n or more characters.

strcat.description: Appends the string src to the end of dest. The destination must have enough space. Unsafe — prefer strncat.

strncat.description: Appends at most n characters from src to dest, then appends a null terminator. The destination must have enough space for the result.

strcmp.description: Compares two strings lexicographically. Returns 0 if equal, a negative value if s1 < s2, or a positive value if s1 > s2.

strncmp.signature: int strncmp(const char *s1, const char *s2, size_t n)
strncmp.description: Compares up to n characters of two strings. Returns 0 if the first n characters are equal.

strchr.description: Returns a pointer to the first occurrence of character c in string s, or NULL if not found. The terminating null byte is considered part of the string.

strrchr.description: Returns a pointer to the last occurrence of character c in string s, or NULL if not found.

strstr.description: Finds the first occurrence of the substring needle in the string haystack. Returns a pointer to the beginning of the match, or NULL if not found.

strtok.description: Splits a string into tokens. The first call takes the string; subsequent calls with NULL continue tokenizing. Modifies the original string. Not thread-safe — use strtok_r instead.

memcpy.description: Copies n bytes from src to dest. The memory regions must not overlap — use memmove for overlapping regions. Returns dest.

memmove.description: Copies n bytes from src to dest, correctly handling overlapping memory regions. Returns dest.

memset.description: Fills the first n bytes of the memory area pointed to by s with the byte value c. Returns s. Commonly used to zero-initialize buffers.

memcmp.signature: int memcmp(const void *s1, const void *s2, size_t n)
memcmp.description: Compares the first n bytes of two memory areas. Returns 0 if equal, a negative value if s1 < s2, or a positive value if s1 > s2.

sqrt.description: Computes the non-negative square root of x. Domain error if x is negative. Link with -lm.

pow.description: Computes base raised to the power exp. Domain error if base is negative and exp is not an integer. Link with -lm.

fabs.description: Returns the absolute value of the floating-point number x.

ceil.description: Returns the smallest integer value not less than x (round up toward positive infinity).

floor.description: Returns the largest integer value not greater than x (round down toward negative infinity).

round.description: Rounds x to the nearest integer, halfway cases away from zero. C99.

sin.description: Computes the sine of x, where x is in radians.

cos.description: Computes the cosine of x, where x is in radians.

tan.description: Computes the tangent of x, where x is in radians.

log.description: Computes the natural (base-e) logarithm of x. Domain error if x is negative; pole error if x is zero.

log10.description: Computes the base-10 logarithm of x. Domain error if x is negative; pole error if x is zero.

exp.description: Computes e raised to the power x. Range error on overflow.

asin.description: Computes the arc sine of x. The result is in the range [-pi/2, pi/2]. Domain error if x is outside [-1, 1].

acos.description: Computes the arc cosine of x. The result is in the range [0, pi]. Domain error if x is outside [-1, 1].

atan.description: Computes the arc tangent of x. The result is in the range [-pi/2, pi/2].

atan2.description: Computes the arc tangent of y/x, using the signs of both arguments to determine the quadrant. The result is in the range [-pi, pi].

isalpha.description: Tests whether c is an alphabetic character (a-z, A-Z). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

isdigit.description: Tests whether c is a decimal digit (0-9). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

isalnum.description: Tests whether c is alphanumeric (letter or digit). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

isspace.description: Tests whether c is a whitespace character (space, tab, newline, etc.). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

isupper.description: Tests whether c is an uppercase letter (A-Z). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

islower.description: Tests whether c is a lowercase letter (a-z). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

toupper.description: Converts a lowercase letter to uppercase. If c is not a lowercase letter, it is returned unchanged.

tolower.description: Converts an uppercase letter to lowercase. If c is not an uppercase letter, it is returned unchanged.

isprint.description: Tests whether c is a printable character, including space. Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

ispunct.description: Tests whether c is a punctuation character (printable, not space, not alphanumeric). Returns non-zero if true, 0 if false.

time.description: Returns the current calendar time as a time_t value, or (time_t)-1 on error. If tloc is not NULL, the return value is also stored at *tloc.

clock.description: Returns the processor time consumed by the program so far, or (clock_t)-1 if unavailable. Divide by CLOCKS_PER_SEC to get seconds.

difftime.description: Returns the difference between two time_t values (time1 - time0) as a double in seconds.

mktime.description: Converts a broken-down time (struct tm) to a time_t calendar time. Normalizes the fields of timeptr. Returns (time_t)-1 on error.

localtime.description: Converts a time_t value to a broken-down time in the local timezone. Returns a pointer to a static struct tm (not thread-safe — use localtime_r).

gmtime.description: Converts a time_t value to a broken-down time in UTC. Returns a pointer to a static struct tm (not thread-safe — use gmtime_r).

strftime.signature: size_t strftime(char *s, size_t max, const char *format, const struct tm *tm)
strftime.description: Formats a broken-down time according to the format string. Format specifiers: %Y (year), %m (month), %d (day), %H (hour), %M (minute), %S (second), %A (weekday name), etc.

ctime.description: Converts a time_t value to a human-readable string in the form "Wed Jun 30 21:49:08 1993\n". Returns a pointer to a static buffer.

signal.description: Sets the disposition of signal signum to handler: SIG_DFL (default), SIG_IGN (ignore), or a custom function. Returns the previous handler, or SIG_ERR on error.

raise.description: Sends signal sig to the calling process. Returns 0 on success, non-zero on failure. Common signals: SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGABRT, SIGSEGV.

assert.description: Macro that aborts the program with a diagnostic message if the expression evaluates to 0 (false). Disabled when NDEBUG is defined.

errno.description: Thread-local error number variable set by system calls and library functions on error. Common values: EACCES (permission denied), ENOENT (no such file), ENOMEM (out of memory), EINVAL (invalid argument).

strerror.description: Returns a pointer to a string describing the error code errnum. The returned string should not be modified.

FILE.description: Opaque type representing a file stream. Used with all stdio.h file I/O functions. Standard streams: stdin, stdout, stderr.

size_t.description: Unsigned integer type returned by sizeof and used for memory sizes and counts throughout the standard library. Guaranteed to hold the size of any object.

ptrdiff_t.description: Signed integer type representing the difference between two pointers. Result of subtracting one pointer from another.

int8_t.description: Exact-width signed 8-bit integer type. Range: -128 to 127.

uint8_t.description: Exact-width unsigned 8-bit integer type. Range: 0 to 255.

int16_t.description: Exact-width signed 16-bit integer type. Range: -32768 to 32767.

uint16_t.description: Exact-width unsigned 16-bit integer type. Range: 0 to 65535.

int32_t.description: Exact-width signed 32-bit integer type. Range: -2147483648 to 2147483647.

uint32_t.description: Exact-width unsigned 32-bit integer type. Range: 0 to 4294967295.

int64_t.description: Exact-width signed 64-bit integer type. Range: -9223372036854775808 to 9223372036854775807.

uint64_t.description: Exact-width unsigned 64-bit integer type. Range: 0 to 18446744073709551615.

intptr_t.description: Signed integer type capable of holding a pointer value. Can safely cast a void* to intptr_t and back.

uintptr_t.description: Unsigned integer type capable of holding a pointer value. Can safely cast a void* to uintptr_t and back.

time_t.description: Arithmetic type representing calendar time. Typically the number of seconds since the Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC).

clock_t.description: Arithmetic type representing processor time. Divide by CLOCKS_PER_SEC to convert to seconds.

struct tm.description: Broken-down time structure representing calendar date and time components.

tm_sec.description: Seconds after the minute [0, 60] (60 for leap second)

tm_isdst.description: Daylight saving time flag (>0 if DST, 0 if not, <0 if unknown)

div_t.signature: typedef struct { int quot; int rem; } div_t
div_t.description: Structure returned by div() containing the quotient and remainder of integer division.

NULL.description: Null pointer constant. Defined in stddef.h, stdio.h, stdlib.h, string.h and other headers. Tests for a null pointer: if (ptr == NULL) or if (!ptr).

EOF.description: End-of-file indicator, a negative integer constant (typically -1). Returned by character input functions to indicate end of input or error.

SEEK_SET.description: File position constant for fseek: offset is relative to the beginning of the file.

SEEK_CUR.description: File position constant for fseek: offset is relative to the current position.

SEEK_END.description: File position constant for fseek: offset is relative to the end of the file.

EXIT_SUCCESS.description: Indicates successful program termination. Passed to exit() or returned from main().

EXIT_FAILURE.description: Indicates unsuccessful program termination. Passed to exit() or returned from main().

RAND_MAX.description: The maximum value returned by rand(). At least 32767.

BUFSIZ.description: Default buffer size for setbuf. Typically 512 or 8192 bytes.

stdin.description: Standard input stream. Connected to keyboard input by default.

stdout.description: Standard output stream. Connected to terminal output by default. Line-buffered when connected to a terminal.

stderr.description: Standard error stream. Connected to terminal output by default. Unbuffered for immediate error reporting.
